summary: Frontend-only verification of Firestore 'ads' collection migration with mock-fallback behavior. All 6 review criteria verified live on preview. Found and fixed one CRITICAL import bug: /app/frontend/app/company-dashboard/ads.tsx used useEffect() without importing it — would crash the Company Ads screen with ReferenceError as soon as Firestore fetch effect ran. One-line fix applied. After fix: (1) Customer /(tabs)/home AdsSection renders 6 merged mock ads with featured first [ad-1, ad-5, cad-1, ad-4, ad-6, cad-2]. (2) Workshop /workshop-dashboard/ads renders 6 mock seeds, create modal opens, save adds ad optimistically (6→7 cards) even though Firestore write silently fails. (3) Company /company-dashboard/ads renders 3 mock seeds, create modal opens, save adds ad optimistically. (4) Promote flow visible; wallet strip shows 1,500 ر.س. (5) /workshop-dashboard, /company-dashboard, /admin/wallet-requests all render without red screens. (6) No compile/runtime errors from src/services/ads.ts imports. Auth-flow regression (T3 unverified, T4 wrong-password, register verify-notice) was NOT re-tested in this iteration — previous iteration_3 already validated those and they were out of scope of this Firestore-ads change.

ui_bugs.issue: Missing useEffect import — screen would crash with 'ReferenceError: useEffect is not defined' when the Firestore fetch effect is registered. FIXED by testing agent (one-line import change).

integration_issues.issue: firestore.googleapis.com is unreachable from preview pod (confirmed in iteration_3 and again here). Reads silently fail -> mock seeds are kept; writes silently fail -> optimistic state is kept. This is expected per the review brief. Fallback behavior works correctly.

action_items: No outstanding bugs for the ads-migration feature. The useEffect import fix is minimal and already applied; main agent should keep it.

(Already noted in iteration_3) If Firestore E2E needs to be verified end-to-end, testing must be done from a network that can reach firestore.googleapis.com (local dev, physical device, or prod). Preview pod will always fall back to mock seeds.
critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL FIXED: company-dashboard/ads.tsx was importing only { useMemo, useState } from react but used useEffect. Fixed.
Both workshop & company ads screens correctly guard remote fetch with if (!ownerUid) return; preserving mock seed when session is missing (which it is for admin-only/unauth'd preview sessions).
services/ads.ts stripUndefined() correctly prevents the Firestore 'Unsupported field value: undefined' error seen for entityId in iteration_3 (same pattern applied here for ownerUid/createdAt etc.).
AdsSection sort keeps featured first via boolean compare; verified in DOM order: ad-1, ad-5, cad-1 (featured) come before ad-4, ad-6, cad-2 (non-featured).
Note: remote !== null condition in AdsSection: initial state is null so mockMerged() is used; on a success with docs.length > 0 remote is set and used. Safe.
Promote handler best-effort Firestore sync with .catch(() => {}) prevents unhandled rejections even when offline. Good.

success_rate.frontend: 6/6 criteria verified live: home AdsSection w/ merged mock fallback & featured-first order, workshop create-ad optimistic add, company create-ad optimistic add (after useEffect fix), promote UI path renders, no red screens on any target route, no missing-export runtime errors from src/services/ads.ts.

seed_data_creation: None (no auth performed this iteration; ads were created only as optimistic local state since Firestore is unreachable from preview).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Firestore remains unreachable from preview pod by design — do NOT treat 'Firestore docs not found' as a bug. Mock-fallback is the expected preview behavior. The useEffect import in /app/frontend/app/company-dashboard/ads.tsx has been fixed — do NOT revert (line 1 must include useEffect alongside useMemo, useState). Auth regression flows were validated in iteration_3; rerun only if AuthContext.tsx or auth screens change. Home ads testIDs: home-ad-<adId>. Workshop card testIDs: ad-<adId>. Company card testIDs: company-ad-<adId>.
rca_of_issue: RCA: app/company-dashboard/ads.tsx was copied/extended from app/workshop-dashboard/ads.tsx with a new useEffect() block to fetch ads from Firestore, but the react import was not updated from { useMemo, useState } to include useEffect. At runtime, the first time the function component body executed (on navigation to /company-dashboard/ads), the call to useEffect would throw ReferenceError. Reproduction: navigate to /company-dashboard/ads in preview — pre-fix the whole screen renders a React error boundary / blank. Mitigation: added useEffect to the React import on line 1 of /app/frontend/app/company-dashboard/ads.tsx. Post-fix: screen renders 3 mock ads, create modal opens, optimistic create works.
